author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xix. 17, ‘If thou wouldest enter into life, keep the commandments.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xix. 17, ‘If thou wouldest enter into life, keep the commandments.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Gospel lesson which has now sounded in our ears, Brethren, requires rather an attentive hearer and a doer, than an expositor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is more clear than this light, “If thou wilt enter into life, keep the commandments”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then have I to say but, “If thou wilt enter into life, keep the commandments”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is there that does not wish for life? and yet who is there that does wish to keep the commandments?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou dost not wish to keep the commandments, why seekest thou after life?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou art slow to the work, why dost thou hasten to the reward?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The rich young man in the Gospel said that he had kept the commandments; then he heard the greater precepts, “If thou wilt be perfect, one thing is lacking to thee, go sell all that thou hast, and give to the poor;” thou shalt not lose them, but “thou shalt have treasure in heaven; and come and follow Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what shall it profit thee, if thou shalt do all the rest, and yet not follow Me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But as ye have heard, “he went away” sad and “sorrowful; for he had great riches.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What he heard, have we heard also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Gospel is Christ’s voice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He sitteth in heaven; but He doth not cease to speak on earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not be deaf, for He is crying out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not be dead; for He is thundering.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou wilt not do the greater things, do at least the less.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the burden of the greater be too much for thee, at least take up the less.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why art thou slow to both? why settest thyself against both?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The greater are, “Sell all that thou hast, and give to the poor, and follow Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The less are, “Thou shalt do no murder, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not steal, Thou shall not bear false witness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Honour thy father and thy mother; and, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These do; why do I call to thee, to sell thy possessions, from whom I cannot gain, that thou wouldest keep from plundering what is another’s?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast heard, “Thou shalt not steal;” yet thou dost plunder.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Before the eyes of so great a Judge, I find thee not a thief only, but a plunderer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Spare thyself, have pity on thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This life yet allows thee respite, do not refuse correction.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yesterday thou wast a thief; be not so to-day too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or if peradventure thou hast been so to-day already, be not so to-morrow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Put a stop sometime to thy evil doing, and so require good for a reward.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wouldest have good things, and wouldest not be good; thy life is a contradiction to thy desires.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If to have a good country-seat, is a great good: how great an evil must it be to have an evil soul!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The rich man “went away sorrowful;” and the Lord said, “How hardly shall he that hath riches enter into the kingdom of heaven!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And by putting forth a comparison He showed the difficulty to be such that it was absolutely impossible.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For every impossible thing is difficult; but not every difficult thing is impossible.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As to how difficult it is, take heed to the comparison; “Verily I say unto you, It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A camel to go through the eye of a needle!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If He had said a gnat, it would be impossible.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then when His disciples heard it, they were grieved and said, “If this be so, who then can be saved?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What rich man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give ear then to Christ, ye poor, I am speaking to the people of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are more of you poor than rich, do ye then at least receive what I say, yet give heed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whosoever of you boast of your poverty, beware of pride, lest the humble rich surpass you; beware of impiety, lest the pious rich surpass you; beware of drunkenness, lest the sober rich surpass you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not glory of your poverty, if they must not glory of their riches.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And let the rich give ear, if indeed they are rich; let them give ear to the Apostle, “Charge the rich of this world,” for there are who are the rich of another world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The poor are the rich of another world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostles are the rich of another world, who said, “As having nothing, and yet possessing all things.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So that ye may know of what poor he is speaking he added, “of this world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the “rich” then “of this world” give ear to the Apostle, “Charge,” he says, “the rich of this world, that they be not proud in their conceits.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The first worm of riches is pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A consuming moth, which gnaws the whole, and reduces it even to dust.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Charge them,” therefore, “not to be proud in their conceits, nor to trust in the uncertainty of riches” (they are the Apostle’s words), “but in the living God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A thief may take away thy gold; who can take away thy God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What hath the rich man, if he hath not God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What hath the poor man not, if he have God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore he says, “Nor to trust in riches, but in the living God, who giveth us all things richly to enjoy;” with which all things He giveth also Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then they ought not to “trust in riches,” not to confide in them, “but in the living God;” what are they to do with their riches?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear what: “Let them be rich in good works.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does this mean?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Explain, O Apostle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For many are loth to understand what they are loth to practise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Explain, O Apostle; give none occasion to evil works by the obscurity of thy words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell us what thou dost mean by, “let them be rich in good works.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them hear and understand; let them not be suffered to excuse themselves; but rather let them begin to accuse themselves, and to say what we have just heard in the Psalm, “For I acknowledge my sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell us what this is, “let them be rich in good works.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them easily distribute.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what is “let them easily distribute”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! is this too not understood?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let them easily distribute, let them communicate.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast, another hath not: communicate, that God may communicate to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Communicate here, and thou shalt communicate there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Communicate thy bread here, and thou shalt receive Bread there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What bread here?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That which thou dost gather with sweat and toil, according to the curse upon the first man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What Bread there?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Even Him who said, “I am the Living Bread which came down from heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here thou art rich, but thou art poor there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Gold thou hast, but thou hast not yet the Presence of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lay out what thou hast, that thou mayest receive what thou hast not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let them be rich in good works, let them easily distribute, let them communicate.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Must they then lose all they have?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He said, “Let them communicate,” not “Let them give the whole.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them keep for themselves as much as is sufficient for them, let them keep more than is sufficient.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us give a certain portion of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What portion?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Scribes and Pharisees gave tithes for whom Christ had not yet shed His Blood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Scribes and Pharisees gave tithes; lest haply thou shouldest think thou art doing any great thing in breaking thy bread to the poor; and this is scarcely a thousandth part of thy means.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet I am not finding fault with this; do even this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So hungry and thirsty am I, that I am glad even of these crumbs.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But yet I cannot keep back what He who died for us said whilst He was alive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Except your righteousness exceed the righteousness of the Scribes and Pharisees, ye shall in no case enter into the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He does not deal softly with us; for He is a physician, He cuts to the quick.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Except your righteousness exceed the righteousness of the Scribes and Pharisees, ye shall in no case enter into the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Scribes and Pharisees gave the tenth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How is it with you?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ask yourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider what you do, and with what means you do it; how much you give, how much you leave for yourselves; what you spend on mercy, what you reserve for luxury.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, “Let them distribute easily, let them communicate, let them lay up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may hold on eternal life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have admonished the rich; now hear, ye poor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye rich, lay out your money; ye poor, refrain from plundering.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye rich, distribute your means; ye poor, bridle your desires.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear, ye poor, this same Apostle; “Godliness with sufficiency is a great getting.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Getting is the acquiring of gain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The world is yours in common with the rich; ye have not a house in common with the rich, but ye have the heaven in common, the light in common.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seek only for a sufficiency, seek for what is enough, and do not wish for more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All the rest is a weight, rather than a help; a burden, rather than an honour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Godliness with sufficiency is great gain.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: First is Godliness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Godliness is the worship of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Godliness with sufficiency.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we brought nothing into this world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Didst thou bring anything hither?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nay, not even did ye rich bring anything.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye found all here, ye were born naked as the poor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In both alike is the same bodily infirmity; the same infant crying, the witness of our misery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For we brought nothing into this world” (he is speaking to the poor), “neither can we carry anything out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And having food and covering, let us be therewith content.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For they who wish to be rich.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Who wish to be,” not who are.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they who are so, well and good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They have heard their lesson, that they be “rich in good works, that they distribute easily, that they communicate.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They have heard already.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do ye now hear who are not yet rich.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “They who wish to be rich, fall into temptation and a snare, and into many hurtful and foolish lusts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do ye not fear?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear what follows; “which drown men in destruction and perdition.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou not now fear? “for avarice is the root of all evil”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Avarice is the wishing to be rich, not the being rich already.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is avarice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou not fear to be “drowned in destruction and perdition”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou not fear “avarice the root of all evil”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou pluckest up out of thy field the root of thorns, and wilt thou not pluck up out of thy heart the root of evil desires?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou cleansest thy field from which thy body gets its fruit, and wilt thou not cleanse thy heart where thy God indwelleth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For avarice is the root of all evil, which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith, and entangled themselves in many sorrows.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have now heard what ye must do, ye have heard what ye must fear, ye have heard how the kingdom of heaven may be purchased, ye have heard by what the kingdom of heaven may be hindered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be ye all of one mind in obeying the word of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God made both the rich and poor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Scripture says, “The rich and the poor meet together, the Lord is the Maker of them both.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The rich and the poor meet together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In what way, except in this present life?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The rich and the poor are born alike.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye meet one another as ye walk on the way together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not thou oppress, nor thou defraud.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The one hath need, the other hath plenty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But “the Lord is the Maker of them both.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By him who hath, He helpeth him that needeth; by him who hath not, He proveth him that hath.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard, we have spoken; let us fear, let us take heed, let us pray, let us attain.
